title: rubus × immanis
intro: Rubus × immanis, also known as the Himalayan blackberry or thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the Himalayan region. The plant's leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are effective in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus × immanis include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
